personalInfo.name: Karima Echihabi
personalInfo.title: Assistant Professor x2 of Computer Science
personalInfo.tagline: Advancing Responsible and Scalable Data Science for Societal Impact
personalInfo.bio: Prof. Karima Echihabi is an Assistant Professor of Computer Science at Mohammed VI Polytechnic University in Morocco (UM6P). Her research interests lie in responsible and scalable data science, spanning topics in data management, machine learning, high-performance computing, and socio-legal studies. She focuses on similarity search as a key operation in critical data science tasks such as data cleaning, data integration, and information retrieval. She is the founding faculty advisor for the UM6P ACM Student Chapter, a senior member of the IEEE, and an Associate Editor for ACM SIGMOD and PVLDB. Before joining UM6P, she worked as a software engineer at Microsoft Redmond and IBM Toronto Lab, and ran her own consultancy business.

elements.welcomeMessage: Welcome to my academic portfolio. I'm passionate about developing responsible and scalable data science solutions that address real-world challenges.
highlights: Associate Editor for ACM SIGMOD and PVLDB
Founding Faculty Advisor for UM6P ACM Student Chapter
Gold Medal coach at 2024 UNESCO/IRCAI AI in Africa Competition
Publications in top venues: ACM SIGMOD, CACM, ICDE, PVLDB

elements.title: Scalable Similarity Search for High-Dimensional Data
elements.description: Designing scalable similarity search techniques for massive collections of high-dimensional vectors and exploiting them to achieve similarity-based data integration.

elements.title: Elpis: Graph-Based Similarity Search for Scalable Data Science
elements.description: Graph-based approach to similarity search that enables scalable data science operations on large datasets.

elements.title: ProS: Progressive k-NN Search with Probabilistic Quality Guarantees
elements.description: Data series progressive k-NN similarity search and classification system with probabilistic quality guarantees for real-time applications.

elements.title: Hercules: Robust Data Series Similarity Search
elements.description: Advanced similarity search system designed to handle the complexity and scale of modern data series analysis.

elements.title: Quality Assessment of Sentiment Analysis Tools
elements.description: Comprehensive evaluation of sentiment analysis tools examining the reasons for inconsistency and proposing quality assessment frameworks.

elements.title: Scalable Machine Learning on High-Dimensional Vectors
elements.description: Techniques for scalable machine learning operations on high-dimensional data, from data series to deep network embeddings.

elements.title: The Lernaean Hydra of Data Series Similarity Search
elements.description: Comprehensive experimental evaluation of the state-of-the-art in data series similarity search, identifying key challenges and opportunities.
Editorial responsibilities for premier database and data management conferences, overseeing peer review process and maintaining publication standards.
Recognition of professional accomplishments and contributions to the field of computer science and engineering.
Establishing and guiding the first ACM student chapter at Mohammed VI Polytechnic University, mentoring students in professional development.

elements.organization: The 9th International Conference on Network Systems (NETYS)
elements.description: Led program committee for international conference on networked systems, coordinating review process and program organization.
Regular program committee service for top-tier database and data management conferences.

elements.organization: VLDB Journal, TKDE, SIGMOD, VLDB, DMKD
elements.description: Peer review activities for premier journals and conferences in database systems and data management.
Editorial responsibilities for conference proceedings in networked systems research.

teaching.name: Teaching

elements.courseName: Data Science: Data Storage and Management
elements.description: Advanced course covering modern data storage architectures, data management systems, and scalable data processing techniques for data science applications.
Comprehensive introduction to data mining techniques, algorithms, and applications including pattern recognition, classification, clustering, and association rules.
Business intelligence systems design, data warehousing, OLAP operations, and decision support systems for enterprise environments.
Advanced concepts in database systems including distributed databases, NoSQL systems, and modern data management architectures.
Fundamentals of programming using C language, covering basic algorithms, data structures, and problem-solving techniques.
Foundation course in computational thinking, problem decomposition, pattern recognition, and algorithmic design principles.

teaching.philosophy: I believe in combining theoretical foundations with practical applications, emphasizing hands-on learning through real-world projects. My teaching approach integrates industry experience with academic rigor to prepare students for both research and professional careers.

elements.name: Hasnae Zerouaoui
elements.bio: Microsoft PhD Fellowship recipient specializing in advanced machine learning techniques and their applications to large-scale data science problems.

elements.name: Ilias Azizi
elements.bio: Co-supervised with Prof. Themis Palpanas at Université de Paris. Focus on graph-based approaches to scalable similarity search for data science applications.

elements.name: Khaoula Abdenouri
elements.bio: Research focus on ethical and responsible approaches to data science, with emphasis on fairness and transparency in machine learning systems.

elements.name: Anas Ait Aomar
elements.bio: Oracle Zurich funded PhD student working on high-performance computing solutions for large-scale data science applications.

elements.name: Rida Lefdali
elements.bio: Research engineer specializing in developing scalable systems and implementing research prototypes for similarity search and data management.

elements.name: Mohamed Jarat
elements.currentPosition: Senior Software Engineer at Tech Company
elements.bio: Former research engineer who contributed to multiple projects in data systems implementation and performance optimization.
